project.title: Counterfactual Audio
project.paper_title: Learning Audio Concepts from Counterfactual Natural Language
project.summary: Counterfactual caption release for AudioCaps, Clotho, and MACS, prepared for the ICASSP 2024 paper on audio-language learning with alternative linguistic scenarios.
project.retrieval_gain: 43%+ top-1 improvement on Clotho text-to-audio retrieval

prompts: Audio signal is a kind of wave that is directional from the source to microphone. Then, microphone records the sound. Each sound may have multiple sound sources. Humans detect these sounds and describe it based on its sources. What are the sound source or sources in the given caption?
Alter these sources with something very different and generate a caption of the same length and in one line and without itemization.

datasets.name: AudioCaps
datasets.notes: One factual caption and one counterfactual caption per clip.

examples.factual: Idling car, train blows horn and passes
examples.counterfactual: Dogs barking, thunder rumbles in the distance

examples.factual: A racing vehicle engine is heard passing by
examples.counterfactual: A group of children laughing and playing in a park.

examples.factual: A waterfall is pouring into a stream
examples.counterfactual: A guitar is being strummed in the studio.

examples.factual: Water flows as a child shouts in the background
examples.counterfactual: Wind howls as a guitar strums in the distance.

datasets.name: Clotho
datasets.notes: Five factual captions and five aligned counterfactual captions per clip.

examples.factual: A muddled noise of broken channel of the TV
examples.counterfactual: A tranquil symphony of ocean waves and seagulls at the beach.

examples.factual: A stick strikes a percussive instrument creating reverb
examples.counterfactual: A gentle breeze whispers through the leaves creating a soothing rustle.

examples.factual: The squeaking of an old door opening, rustling ensues, the door squeaks again, and a woman speaks.
examples.counterfactual: The ringing of church bells, chirping of birds, rustling of leaves, and a man whistles.

examples.factual: Dishes click and clack in the background as a crowd chatters indistinctly.
examples.counterfactual: Sirens wail and helicopters thud in the foreground as a journalist interviews a witness.

datasets.name: Clotho
datasets.notes: In the current release, captions_counterfactual matches captions for every paired entry.

datasets.name: MACS
datasets.notes: Multi-annotator captions paired with aligned counterfactual rewrites.

examples.counterfactual: A violin playing and a clock ticking.

examples.factual: adults talking and kids screaming in the background at the beginning a rattling sound occurs
examples.counterfactual: musical instruments playing and people singing in the background at the beginning a phone ringing sound occurs

examples.factual: adults talking with cars accelerating in the background
examples.counterfactual: Musicians jamming with dogs barking in the background.
